2605.22909.title: Sample-efficient benchmarking of shallow all-to-all random quantum circuits
2605.22909.summary:
- Nonlinear cross-entropy is a sample-efficient benchmark for shallow-depth random quantum circuits.
- It separates noisy quantum computers from classical spoofers even with depolarizing noise.
- A binary classifier based on heavy output generation has logarithmic sample complexity at short depth.

2605.22915.title: Unified resonant-manifold framework for dynamical quantum phase transitions
2605.22915.summary:
- A unified framework explains dynamical quantum phase transitions (DQPTs) through energy structure of product state configurations.
- Manifold DQPTs are governed by resonances within the initial state, while branch DQPTs involve resonances with a transitional manifold.
- Branch DQPTs' regularity is linked to the multiplicity of the transitional manifold.

2605.22917.title: Quantum Fisher Information under decoherence with explicit wavefunctions
2605.22917.summary:
- A method to estimate quantum Fisher information under decoherence is presented.
- The method uses Markov-chain Monte Carlo sampling for efficient estimation.
- The framework is applied to Jastrow-Gutzwiller wave functions and noise channels.

2605.22920.title: Estimating Green's functions with a robust quantum Arnoldi method
2605.22920.summary:
- A robust quantum Arnoldi method (ROQAM) is introduced for estimating Green's functions.
- ROQAM preserves the upper-Hessenberg structure of projected matrices through orthogonal polynomials.
- ROQAM outperforms pointwise estimation by multiple orders of magnitude for certain models.

2605.22922.title: Observation of associative-memory retrieval and spin-glass phases on a photonic quantum simulator
2605.22922.summary:
- Researchers used photonic quantum simulators to study associative memories and neural networks.
- The study identified three phases: memory retrieval, spin-glass "black-out," and paramagnetic.
- Experimental results confirmed successful memory retrieval at low storage capacities and temperatures.

2605.22947.title: Entanglement-facilitated macroscopic cluster formation in quantum many-body dynamics
2605.22947.summary:
- Entanglement suppresses fragmentation in quantum many-body systems.
- Initial-state entanglement stabilizes macroscopic connected clusters.
- Entanglement entropy is not the sole factor in stabilizing global structures.

2605.22953.title: Chaos to Synchronization and Dissipative Quantum Scarring in Open Coupled top-Dicke model in a Lossy Cavity
2605.22953.summary:
- The open coupled-top Dicke model explores non-equilibrium phenomena in an open environment.
- Photon loss induces synchronization and chaos in the model, followed by restoration of coherence.
- Two types of quantum scarring are identified, with one remaining protected and the other decaying due to dissipation.

2605.22966.title: Phase-dependent role of dissipation across the Aubry-Andr\'e-Harper transition
2605.22966.summary:
- Bath memory affects transport differently in extended and localized phases.
- In the extended phase, bath memory reshapes the dynamical generator.
- In the localized phase, bath memory mainly renormalizes timescales.

2605.22977.title: Absorbing Many-Body Correlations into Core-Optimized Orbitals
2605.22977.summary:
- The paper proposes a method called Core-Optimized Orbitals (COO) to reduce the computational cost of simulating quantum many-body systems.
- COO absorbs many-body correlations into the single-particle basis, making Configuration Interaction (CI) more parameter-efficient.
- The method achieves significant compactness gains compared to existing methods, such as TrimCI and SU(-adapted DMRG.

2605.22980.title: Automatic De-Quantization of Quantum Programs Using Constant Propagation
2605.22980.summary:
- Researchers propose a method to de-quantize quantum operations for classical computation.
- Hybrid quantum-classical constant propagation reduces quantum operations by trading them for classical instructions.
- The approach makes quantum programs more practical and robust for near-term devices.

2605.22933.title: Emergent heavy-tailed distributions from a Markovian random walk
2605.22933.summary:
- A Markovian random walk with a deterministic step length function exhibits heavy-tailed distributions.
- The walk's stationary state is a Lorentz-like distribution with power-law tails decaying as |x|^{-2}.
- Non-zero initial acceleration drives the walker away from the origin, enabling scale-free statistics.

2605.23228.title: Exact solution of generalized gauge-invariant Ising chains with multi-spin interactions
2605.23228.summary:
- Exact solutions are found for generalized gauge-invariant n-chain Ising models with multi-spin interactions.
- An explicit expression for the partition function is derived using the transfer-matrix method.
- The behavior of the Wilson loop is analyzed, revealing area-law and perimeter-law regimes.

2605.23364.title: Inviscid scaling in the Kuramoto-Sivashinsky equation from functional renormalization group and direct numerical simulations
2605.23364.summary:
- The Kuramoto-Sivashinsky equation features a scaling regime with z=1 at intermediate scales.
- This regime arises from the vanishing of effective viscosity in the equation.
- The behavior pertains to the inviscid-Burgers universality class.

2605.23758.title: Order-Disorder Tricriticality in $\mathrm{A}_n \mathrm{B}_n$ Star Polymer Melts
2605.23758.summary:
- Tricriticality occurs in symmetric $\mathrm{A}_n\mathrm{B}_n$ star-polymer melts due to arm number $n$.
- A tricritical arm number $n_{\mathrm{tc}}\approx 4475$ is identified.
- The transition becomes first order for $nn_{\mathrm{tc}}$ with a quadratic dependence near the tricritical point.

2605.23781.title: Multi-field Return Point Memory
2605.23781.summary:
- Researchers developed a method for controlling non-equilibrium systems with multiple control fields.
- The method, called multi-field return point memory, restores a system to a previous exact microstate.
- This approach enables precise and complex control of the system with commutative and noncommutative behavior.

2605.23789.title: Orientable Surfactants on Thin Liquid Films: A Dynamic Density-Functional Theory Approach
2605.23789.summary:
- Surfactants are treated as polar uniaxial particles in a dynamic density-functional theory approach.
- The model accounts for the shape and amphiphilic nature of surfactants.
- The approach preserves gradient dynamics form with a revised free energy expression.

2605.23803.title: Chirality-sensitive mobility and dissipation of Brownian motion on a helical landscape
2605.23803.summary:
- A particle's Brownian motion on a helical landscape separates into free and harmonic motion under isotropic damping.
- Anisotropic damping destroys separability, requiring a linear Ornstein-Uhlenbeck process in phase space.
- The system's stationary dynamics can be projected onto a stable subspace to obtain a linear response theory.

2605.22906.title: Weak wave turbulence as a precursor to universal coarsening in a homogeneous Bose gas
2605.22906.summary:
- A homogeneous Bose gas exhibits universal coarsening through phase-ordering kinetics.
- Weak wave turbulence describes the initial transport of particles to low momenta.
- The momentum distribution follows a power-law with exponent γ = 4(.

2605.22982.title: Transient and asymptotic Taylor--Aris dispersion of Brownian rods in arbitrary regular-polygonal ducts
2605.22982.summary:
- Taylor-Aris dispersion of Brownian rods in non-circular ducts is influenced by pressure-driven shear and duct geometry.
- A local Jeffery-Brownian closure gives four transport fields at each cross-sectional point in regular-polygonal ducts.
- Rod alignment enhances the Taylor coefficient by reducing transverse mixing, with a small shift in mean speed.

2605.23046.title: Weakly nonlinear interaction of capillary waves in a finite system: leading interaction process and scales' range of direct energy cascade
2605.23046.summary:
- Resonant interactions dominate weakly nonlinear capillary wave interactions.
- Zakharov-Filonenko spectrum forms in isotropic capillary wave turbulence.
- Spectrum's range of scales is finite in finite systems due to wavenumber grid discreteness.

2605.23166.title: Free surfaces in turbulence -- A unified framework from water surfaces to elastic solids
2605.23166.summary:
- A unified framework describes deformable surfaces in turbulent flows.
- The framework distinguishes between surface-driven and flow-driven dynamics.
- Nonlinear interactions and wave turbulence are key components of the framework.

2605.23303.title: On the Applicability of the Gas-Kinetic Scheme with Kinetic Boundary Conditions for Near-Continuum Hypersonic Flows
2605.23303.summary:
- The gas-kinetic scheme (GKS) captures near-equilibrium flow physics using the Chapman-Enskog expansion.
- GKS employs kinetic boundary conditions for velocity slip and temperature jump.
- GKS provides more accurate aerodynamic predictions than conventional CFD solvers in near-continuum flow regimes.

2605.23309.title: Full-component reconstruction of three-dimensional fluid stress tensors
2605.23309.summary:
- Researchers developed U-FlowPET, a framework for reconstructing 3D fluid stress tensors.
- U-FlowPET integrates photoelastic tomography with fluid mechanics equations to reconstruct stress tensors.
- The method validates results using analytical, numerical, and experimental datasets.

2605.23396.title: CHIMERA: A wide Reynolds number range Taylor-Couette facility
2605.23396.summary:
- A Taylor-Couette facility is designed for wide Reynolds number range experiments.
- The facility uses a torsion pendulum for direct torque measurement.
- Measurements cover over five decades in Reynolds number, up to Re ~ 10^

2605.23620.title: From Optical Breakdown to Bubble Inception: A Coupled Plasma-Thermal Framework for Nanosecond Laser-Induced Cavitation in Water
2605.23620.summary:
- A coupled plasma-thermal framework models laser-induced cavitation in water.
- Plasma-induced thermoelastic acoustic relaxation governs early bubble formation.
- The framework accurately captures early cavity formation and bubble expansion.

2605.23800.title: A derivation of viscous thin film flow equations on curved surfaces
2605.23800.summary:
- Viscous thin film flow equations are derived for curved surfaces.
- The derivation includes gravitational, capillary, and Marangoni effects.
- The resulting expression incorporates leading order terms and additional terms for nongeneric cases.

2605.23812.title: An Ensemble Variational approach for High-Dimensional Open-Loop Flow Control
2605.23812.summary:
- An ensemble-variational framework is proposed for high-dimensional flow control.
- It approximates cost-function gradients through a finite ensemble of perturbed control vectors.
- The method is effective in both quasi-periodic and chaotic regimes, reducing kinetic energy fluctuations.

2605.23869.title: Soft Mobility Theory
2605.23869.summary:
- A soft mobility theory is proposed for deformable bodies in viscous flows.
- The theory yields a configuration-dependent ODE for generalized coordinates.
- It enables efficient inverse design and gradient-based optimization.

2605.23776.title: Precipitation diffusion downscaling and application to out-of-distribution simulations with and without stratospheric aerosol injection
2605.23776.summary:
- A deep learning diffusion downscaler is trained to generate high-resolution precipitation data from coarse climate simulations.
- The downscaler accurately recreates climate change projections of extreme precipitation and generates realistic downscaled precipitation.
- Stratospheric aerosol injection (SAI) is modeled to nearly halve the increase in yearly max precipitation in the contiguous United States.

2605.23778.title: The physics of AI weather models
2605.23778.summary:
- AI weather models may be solving physical equations differently than conventional NWP models.
- AI models represent the atmosphere in similar ways despite architectural differences.
- AI models implement a particle description of the atmosphere, with particles moving towards a minimum free energy.

2605.23875.title: Atmosphere as a steam engine
2605.23875.summary:
- Earth's atmosphere operates a steam cycle with water vapor evaporation, expansion, condensation, and precipitation.
- The Clausius-Clapeyron law is generalized to an atmospheric column with condensation over a range of heights.
- Global steam-engine power is estimated as $W_v=4\pm9$ W/m2, close to total atmospheric power.

2605.23403.title: Hybrid Quantum-Classical Corrective Diffusion Modeling for Meteorological Downscaling
2605.23403.summary:
- A hybrid quantum-classical model is proposed for statistical downscaling of weather fields.
- The model combines variational quantum circuit layers with a classical regression branch in a diffusion UNet.
- The hybrid model improves performance over a classical model in several configurations.

2412.00936.title: Smoothing and spatial verification of global fields
2412.00936.summary:
- Two new methodologies for smoothing global fields are presented.
- They can handle irregular grids and missing data.
- Smoothing-based metrics can now be calculated globally.

2503.05331.title: Radiosonde-constrained reconstructions reveal a weakening Northern Hadley circulation
2503.05331.summary:
- Radiosonde data reveal a weakening Northern Hadley circulation since
- Reanalyses systematically underestimate NHC strength, with ERA5 showing persistent strengthening.
- Climate models and radiosonde-based reconstructions agree on a weakening NHC.

2601.05841.title: Non-stationary time series attribution for heatwaves over Europe
2601.05841.summary:
- A non-stationary Markov process models temporal dependence in extreme events.
- Attribution results show strong evidence for anthropogenic climate change over Europe.
- Mean warming's effect is eliminated to examine its influence on attribution statements.

2605.11639.title: Enabling High-Accuracy Data Assimilation with Limited Ensembles via Machine Learning-Based Covariance Correction
2605.11639.summary:
- A machine learning-based EnKF framework improves accuracy with small ensemble sizes.
- A multilayer perceptron predicts covariance difference between limited and large ensembles.
- The predicted difference is incorporated into the EnKF algorithm for improved accuracy.

2605.22901.title: A No-Go Theorem for the Mass-Radius Relation of Solitons
2605.22901.summary:
- A no-go theorem excludes solitons with mass-radius index Γ in the range [0, d].
- The theorem applies to real scalar field theories in d spatial dimensions.
- Typical solitons cannot have Γ in the excluded range due to forces from gradient energy, self-interaction, and gravitation.

2605.23001.title: Nonlinear Wave Propagation in 1D Polycatenated Ring Chains
2605.23001.summary:
- Nonlinear wave propagation is observed in 1D polycatenated ring chains.
- Wave dynamics are driven by tensile activation and internal flexibility.
- System nonlinearity can be tuned through geometric aspect ratio and contact angles.

2605.23688.title: Adjoint inverse design of microresonator frequency combs
2605.23688.summary:
- Adjoint inverse design optimizes microresonator frequency comb spectra directly.
- This approach addresses challenging design problems with high efficiency.
- Inverse design enables compact on-chip light sources tailored to specific applications.

2605.22236.title: Beyond descendants: integrable observables for cohomological field theories
2605.22236.summary:
- Integrable observables are proposed as alternatives to Witten's psi classes in cohomological field theories.
- Three examples of integrable observables are presented, recovering known hierarchies and revealing new features.
- The integrable observables framework yields a proof of the Miura equivalence of new hierarchies and known hierarchies.
